config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan ketika benar-benar menyangkut keselamatan umum, ketertiban, dan keadilan bagi banyak orang. Hak individu tetap penting, tetapi tidak boleh dipakai sebagai alasan untuk merugikan orang lain atau mengorbankan kepentingan bersama. Dalam pandangan saya, keseimbangan yang sehat justru muncul saat kebebasan pribadi dibatasi seperlunya demi kebaikan publik.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective welfare, because those values are deeply familiar and often genuinely important. But I also think that when “society” is used too broadly, it can become an excuse to ignore personal dignity, silence dissent, or justify unfair treatment of individuals. The better principle is that collective interests matter, but they should be limited by clear rights that protect people from abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu; menurut saya itu hanya sah dalam batas yang jelas dan benar-benar diperlukan. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya paham kuatnya nilai gotong royong dan kepentingan bersama, tapi saya juga melihat betapa mudahnya dalih “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk menekan suara kritis atau membenarkan ketidakadilan. Hak individu, terutama martabat, kebebasan berpendapat, dan perlindungan dari perlakuan sewenang-wenang, harus tetap jadi pagar utama. Jadi, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak, melainkan keseimbangan yang ketat: kepentingan umum boleh didahulukan hanya kalau tidak merusak hak dasar orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. Coming from a Chinese background, I understand why people value order, stability, and collective goals, but I have also seen how easily “for the good of society” can be used to pressure individuals and excuse unfairness. A society is strongest when it protects basic rights first, because without that protection, the collective interest can become just the will of the powerful. So I would say the public good matters, but it must never be a blank check to override personal dignity and fundamental freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya memang akrab dengan gotong royong dan rasa kebersamaan, tetapi saya juga tahu dalih “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik dan menutup ketidakadilan. Kepentingan umum itu penting, namun hanya sah jika dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti martabat, kebebasan berpendapat, dan perlindungan dari kesewenang-wenangan. Jadi menurut saya, yang benar bukan mengorbankan individu demi masyarakat, melainkan memastikan masyarakat kuat justru karena hak individu dijaga.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally come before the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of collective goals, social stability, and public order, because those are real and valuable, but I also know how easily “for the good of society” can be used to silence criticism or excuse unfair treatment. A society is not truly strong if it protects the collective by weakening personal dignity, because then the so-called common interest is often just the interest of the powerful. The better standard is that public interests matter, but they must be constrained by clear individual rights that cannot be casually overridden.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
